your_book:
Jaleel's book had a strong tape day, with broad green across the growth names. ServiceNow (NOW) led, up 9.85% to $2,753, followed by Uber (+5.47%), Lululemon (+4.92%), Adobe (+4.82% to $3,176), and Samsara (+7.45%). The account sits at $19,840 with the position still $700 underwater (-3.41%), but today narrowed that gap meaningfully. Cash is essentially nil at $2.49 — no dry powder.

The notable divergences are where price and thesis pull apart. Adobe, the top weight at 16%, beat and raised on Jun 11 yet remains down 17.3% — the AI-moat narrative (Claude Design, CFO exit) is overriding the numbers, and today's bounce doesn't repair that. Rocket Lab (RKLB) is the ugliest: down 19.9%, rated AVOID with a QARP of 39, yet still a 5.5% weight — a low-quality name that's earned a hard look. Boston Scientific and Lululemon both carry elevated guidance-cut flags; BSX slashed FY26 growth and guides flat through Q3, LULU cut on its 5th straight negative Americas comp.

To watch: ABT trimmed its EPS outlook (defensive yellow flag, earnings ~Jul 17), and the pharma cluster (GILD, NVS, NBIX, VRTX) carries an MFN drug-pricing overhang.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated loser still a 5.5% weight
attention.note: Down 19.9% with a QARP of 39 and AVOID verdict — a low-quality holding that contradicts the capital-preservation mandate.

attention.headline: Top weight down 17.3% despite beat-and-raise
attention.note: At 16% of the book, the AI-disruption narrative is driving the stock against improving fundamentals.

attention.headline: FY26 guidance slashed, flat through Q3
attention.note: Growth cut to 7–8.5% with Watchman/EP/urology soft; STRONG BUY rating sits at odds with the guidance reality, earnings ~Jul 23.

attention.headline: Fifth straight negative Americas comp
attention.note: Guidance cut on tariffs and stalled China leaves the thesis weakening even as the stock bounced today.
